content.problemStatement:
Chef gives an array AAA with NNN elements to Babla. Babla's task is to find the **maximum non-negative** integer XXX such that:


No element in the array belongs to the range [−X,X][-X, X][−X,X]. In other words, for all (1≤i≤N)(1\le i \le N)(1≤i≤N), either Ai<−XA_i \lt -XAi​<−X or Ai>XA_i \gt XAi​>X.
Help Babla to find the **maximum non-negative** integer XXX for which the given condition is satisfied or determine if no such XXX exists.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the number of elements in the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ representing the array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum non-negative** integer XXX, satisfying the above condition.

If no such XXX exists, output −1-1−1 instead.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum non‑negative integer X such that no array element lies in the interval [‑X, X]. If no such X exists, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: minimum absolute value is 2, so answer = 2‑1 = 1.\nTest case 2: minimum absolute value is 7, answer = 7‑1 = 6.\nTest case 3: array contains 0, therefore no non‑negative X works, answer = -1.
First test: min|Ai| = 5 → answer 4.\nSecond test: absolute values are 3,8,4 → min = 3 → answer 2.
Minimum absolute value is 1, so the largest X with X < 1 is 0.

solution.approach: For a given test case let m = min{|Ai|}. If m = 0 then the interval [‑X, X] always contains the element 0, so no non‑negative X works and the answer is -1. Otherwise any X with 0 ≤ X < m satisfies the condition, and the largest integer with this property is m‑1. Hence the answer is (minimum absolute value among the array) minus one, or -1 if the minimum absolute value is zero. The algorithm scans the array once, keeping the smallest absolute value seen.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the size of the array. Overall O(total N) ≤ O(2·10^5).
